data.title: Vertical Aerospace completes the first airport-to-airport eVTOL flight of its kind
data.description:
Vertical Aerospace (NYSE:EVTL) announced on Thursday that it completed the world's first airport-to-airport piloted flight by a full-scale, winged tilt-rotor eVTOL designed for commercial service.

The VX4 prototype flew 17 miles from the company's Flight Test Centre at Cotswold Airport to RAF Fairford on July 16, reaching speeds of 115 miles per hour and an altitude of 1,800 feet. The flight followed approvals from the UK Civil Aviation Authority. The inaugural flight was said to showcase integration with real-world airport operations and build momentum toward certified deployment.

Vertical Aerospace (NYSE:EVTL) is showcasing its full-scale VX4 prototype as part of its broader strategy to unlock new hybrid-electric applications in defense, logistics, and special missions. With the recent launch of its hybrid-electric variant, Vertical (NYSE:EVTL) said it is positioned to deliver cleaner, quieter, and more versatile aviation solutions for a range of government and military use cases. 

"Our hybrid-electric roadmap unlocks new capabilities for military operations, and Vertical’s RIAT presence reinforces our commitment to playing a meaningful role in the future of military and special mission aviation," stated CEO Stuart Simpson.

Shares of Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) shot up 7.2% in premarket action.

data.title: Amex Exploration secures up to C$47.6M in financing, Eldorado Gold to acquire strategic stake
data.description:
• Amex Exploration (TSXV:AMX:CA) announced a non-brokered private placement for up to C$24.97M via 11M charity flow-through shares at C$2.27 each.
  • The company may issue an additional 3.125M common shares at C$1.60 for up to C$5M in gross proceeds.
  • Eldorado Gold Corporation (ELD:CA) will invest C$17.6M by purchasing 11M common shares at C$1.60, gaining ~17% ownership of Amex (undiluted).
  • Proceeds will fund exploration and drilling at the Perron Gold Project and Perron West.
  • The offering is expected to close on or before August 7, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: NervGen Pharma announces CEO transition
data.description:
• NervGen Pharma (TSXV:NGEN:CA) announced that chief eexcutive officer Mike Kelly has resigned from his role as director and officer of the company.
  • Adam Rogers, chair of the board and representative of NervGen's largest shareholder, has been appointed interim CEO.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Digi Power X inks deal with Super Micro Computer for Nvidia B200-powered systems
data.description:
• Digi Power X (TSXV:DGX:CA) (NASDAQ:DGXX) shares surged above 30% premarket on Thursday after the AI and digital infrastructure company announced a definitive purchase order with Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) to procure Nvidia B200-powered systems.
  • These high-performance units will support the upcoming launch of NeoCloud, Digi Power X’s Tier 3 modular AI infrastructure platform.
  • The Supermicro systems, pre-qualified for the Blackwell B200 GPUs, will be deployed inside Digi Power X’s proprietary ARMS 200 (AI-Ready Modular Solution) pods. The first live deployment is slated for the company’s Alabama site, with operations expected to begin in the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • “This marks a defining moment in our journey,” said Michel Amar, CEO of Digi Power X. “With Supermicro’s advanced B200 systems, we are now taking the steps to transition from infrastructure buildout to revenue generation. Our goal is to deliver Tier 3 AI-ready capacity equipped with the world’s most powerful GPUs for the generative AI era.”
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GE Aerospace shares set for all-time high on Q2 profit topper, full-year guidance raise
data.description:
GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) +0.8% pre-market Thursday, poised to exceed the stock's all-time intraday high of $266.86 set in the previous session, after beating estimates for Q2 adjusted earnings and revenues, and raising its full-year financial guidance.

Q2 profit jumped to $2.39B, or $1.87/share, from $1.45B, or $1.20/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenues rose 21.2% Y/Y to $11.02B, as profit at the commercial engines and services segment - which generates more than 70% of the company's revenue - surged 33% Y/Y to $2.23B, while revenues rose 30% to $7.99B.

For FY 2025, GE (NYSE:GE) said it now expects adjusted EPS of $5.60-$5.80, up from previous guidance of $5.10-$5.45 and in line with the $5.63 analyst consensus estimate, and raised its outlook for adjusted revenue growth to a "mid-teens" percentage range from low double digits, as well as  operating profit to $8.2B-$8.5B from its prior guidance of $7.8B-$8.2B, and free cash flow to $6.5B-$6.9B from its previous view to $6.3B-$6.8B.

GE (GE) also raised its forecast for 2028, including achieving $11.5B of operating profit and $8.5B of free cash flow, both up $1.5B from its 2024 Investor Day outlook.

The company also said it aims to increase capital returns to shareholders from 2024 to 2026 by 20% to $24B, expecting to sustainably return at least 70% of free cash flow via dividend and buybacks beyond 2026.

data.title: Rails blockbuster: Union Pacific may be looking to acquire CSX or Norfolk Southern
data.description:
Union Pacific Corporation (NYSE:UNP) is weighing a major acquisition, according to SEMFOR. Sources indicate that the company is working with investment bankers at Morgan Stanley.

The report did not reveal the rail giant's target but highlighted that Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) CEO Jim Vena has talked publicly about the virtues of a transcontinental railroad. That could indicate the company is looking at East Coast carriers CSX Corporation (NASDAQ:CSX) or Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC).

A merger would be likely to face major regulatory scrutiny. CSX Corporation (NASDAQ:CSX) has a market cap of over $62 billion, and Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) has a market cap of over $59 billion.

The two largest railroad mergers of all time were the Canadian Pacific-Kansas City Southern combination in 2023 at $31 billion and the Canadian National-Burlington Northern Santa Fe combination in 1999 at $28 billion.

In premarket trading, CSX (CSX) gained 5.4% and Norfolk Southern (NSC) was up 2.6%, while Union Pacific (UNP) was down 2.2%.

data.title: OFG Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.10, revenue of $182.2M beats by $0.52M
data.description:
• OFG Bancorp press release (NYSE:OFG): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.15 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $182.2M (+1.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.52M.
  • Net interest margin of 5.31%, return on average assets of 1.73%, return on average tangible common stockholders’ equity of 16.96%, and efficiency ratio of 52.04%.
  • CET1 ratio was 13.99% compared to 14.27% in 1Q25 and 14.29% in 2Q24. Tangible Common Equity ratio was 10.20% compared to 10.30% in 1Q25 and 10.09% in 2Q24. Tangible Book Value per share was $27.67 compared to $26.66 in 1Q25 and $24.18 in 2Q24.
